title: polygonum fowleri subsp. hudsonianum
intro: Polygonum fowleri subsp. hudsonianum, also known as Hudson's water clover or Hudson's water knotweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the knotweed family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Polygonum fowleri subsp. hudsonianum is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices that can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally to promote overall well-being.
